title: Data Mining for Decision Making - Week 2

questions.question: In a Student–Professor dataset with 6 Students and 2 Professors, the entropy at the root is closest to:
What is the primary goal of constructing a decision tree?
If all samples at a node belong to the same class, the node entropy is:
Which quantity is minimized when selecting the best attribute for a split in a decision tree?
At a node in a decision tree, the entropy is 0.94. After splitting on attribute Habit, the weighted entropy becomes 0.30. What is the Information Gain obtained by splitting on Habit?
Which dataset is used to decide the optimal attribute ordering in a decision tree?
When an attribute value is missing in a decision tree, a common strategy is to:
options: Distribute the instance fractionally among branches
questions.answer: Distribute the instance fractionally among branches

questions.question: Information Gain of an attribute is computed as:
options: Difference between split entropy and root entropy
Root entropy minus weighted split entropy
questions.answer: Root entropy minus weighted split entropy

questions.question: If splitting on an attribute does not change entropy, the Information Gain is:

The perceptron learning rule updates weights using:
What does the learning rate β control in a neural network?
A single-layer perceptron can correctly model which logic gate?
Why can XOR not be modeled by a single perceptron?
Input x = [1, 0], weights w = [0.4, −0.2], bias = −0.1. Using step activation, output is:
Which activation function outputs values in the range (0,1)?
Which activation function outputs values between −1 and +1?
Backpropagation is mainly used to:
In case-based updating, weights are updated:
A two-layer neural network is required to model:
